items.title: alternateIdentifier
items.description: This is the only identifier issued by the IPT for the metadata document; it is a UUID

items.title: title
items.description: The 'title' field provides a description of the resource that is being documented that is long enough to differentiate it from other similar resources. Multiple titles may be provided, particularly when trying to express the title in more than one language (use the "xml:lang" attribute to indicate the language if not English/en).

pubDate.title: pubDate
pubDate.description: The date on which the resource was published

language.title: language
language.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

creator.title: creator
creator.description:
The creator is the person who created the resource (not necessarily the author of this metadata about the resource).
This is the person or institution to contact with questions about the use, interpretation of a dataset.

organizationName.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

individualName.title: individualName
individualName.description: The individualName field contains subfields so that a person's name can be broken down into parts.

givenName.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

address.description: The address field is a container for multiple subfields that describe the physical or electronic address of the responsible party for a resource.

onlineUrl.title: onlineUrl
onlineUrl.description: A link to associated online information, usually a web site. When the party represents an organization, this is the URL to a website or other online information about the organization. If the party is an individual, it might be their personal web site or other related online information about the party.

metadataProvider.description: The party responsible for the creation of the metadata document

role.title: role
role.description: Use this field to describe the role the party played with respect to the resource. Some potential roles include technician, reviewer, principal investigator, and many others.

abstract.title: abstract
abstract.description: A brief overview describing the dataset

para.title: para
para.description: The "paragraph" element allows for text blocks to be included in EML.

items.title: keywordSet
items.description: A wrapper element for the keyword and keywordThesaurus elements

items.title: keyword
items.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

keywordThesaurus.title: keywordThesaurus
keywordThesaurus.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

additionalInfo.title: additionalInfo
additionalInfo.description: Any information that is not characterized by the other resource metadata fields

para.title: para
para.description: The "paragraph" element allows for text blocks to be included in EML.

intellectualRights.title: intellectualRights
intellectualRights.description: Contain a rights management statement for the resource, or reference a service providing such information

para.title: para
para.description: The "paragraph" element allows for text blocks to be included in EML.

distribution.title: distribution
distribution.description: This element provides information on how the resource is distributed. When used at the resource level, this element can provide only general information, but elements for describing connections to online systems are provided.

online.title: online
online.description: This element contains information for accessing the resource online represented as a URL connection

url.title: url
url.description: The URL of the resource that is available online.

items.title: coverage
items.description: Describes the extent of the coverage of the resource in terms of its spatial extent, temporal extent, and taxonomic extent

geographicCoverage.title: geographicCoverage
geographicCoverage.description: A container for spatial information about a resource; allows a bounding box for the overall coverage (in lat long), and also allows description of arbitrary polygons with exclusions.

geographicDescription.title: geographicDescription
geographicDescription.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

boundingCoordinates.title: boundingCoordinates
boundingCoordinates.description: Bounding Coordinates are the four margins (N, S, E, W) of a bounding box, or when considered in lat-lon pairs, the corners of the box.

westBoundingCoordinate.title: westBoundingCoordinate
westBoundingCoordinate.description: The westBoundingCoordinate field defines the longitude of the western-most point of the bounding box that is being described.

eastBoundingCoordinate.title: eastBoundingCoordinate
eastBoundingCoordinate.description: The eastBoundingCoordinate field defines the longitude of the eastern-most point of the bounding box that is being described.

northBoundingCoordinate.title: northBoundingCoordinate
northBoundingCoordinate.description: The northBoundingCoordinate field defines the latitude of the northern-most point of the bounding box that is being described.

southBoundingCoordinate.title: southBoundingCoordinate
southBoundingCoordinate.description: The southBoundingCoordinate field defines the latitude of the southern-most point of the bounding box that is being described.

temporalCoverage.title: temporalCoverage
temporalCoverage.description: This field specifies temporal coverage, and allows coverages to be a single point in time, multiple points in time, or a range of dates.

rangeOfDates.title: rangeOfDates
rangeOfDates.description: The 'RangeOfDates' field is intended to be used for describing a range of dates and/or times. It may be used multiple times to document multiple date ranges. It allows for two 'singleDateTime' fields, the first to be used as the beginning dateTime, and the second to be used as the ending dateTime of the range.

beginDate.title: beginDate
beginDate.description: A single time stamp signifying the beginning of some time period

calendarDate2.title: calendarDate2
calendarDate2.description: The date on which the resource was published

endDate.title: endDate
endDate.description: A single time stamp signifying the end of some time period

calendarDate2.title: calendarDate2
calendarDate2.description: The date on which the resource was published

singleDateTime.title: singleDateTime
singleDateTime.description: The SingleDateTime field is intended to describe a single date and time for an event

calendarDate2.title: calendarDate2
calendarDate2.description: The date on which the resource was published

taxonomicCoverage.title: taxonomicCoverage
taxonomicCoverage.description: Taxonomic Coverage is a container for taxonomic information about a resource. It includes a list of species names (or higher level ranks) from one or more classification systems.

generalTaxonomicCoverage.title: generalTaxonomicCoverage
generalTaxonomicCoverage.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

items.title: taxonomicClassification
items.description: Information about the range of taxa addressed in the dataset or collection

taxonRankName.title: taxonRankName
taxonRankName.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

taxonRankValue.title: taxonRankValue
taxonRankValue.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

commonName.title: commonName
commonName.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

purpose.title: purpose
purpose.description: A description of the purpose of the resource/dataset

para.description: The "paragraph" element allows for text blocks to be included in EML.

onlineUrl.title: onlineUrl
onlineUrl.description: A link to associated online information, usually a web site. When the party represents an organization, this is the URL to a website or other online information about the organization. If the party is an individual, it might be their personal web site or other related online information about the party.

items.title: methods
items.description: The methods field documents scientific methods used in the collection of this dataset. It includes information on items such as tools, instrument calibration and software.

methodStep.title: methodStep
methodStep.description: The methodStep field allows for repeated sets of elements that document a series of procedures followed to produce a data object. These include text descriptions of the procedures, relevant literature, software, instrumentation, source data and any quality control measures taken.

description.title: description
description.description: The field Description contains general textual descriptions.

qualityControl.title: qualityControl
qualityControl.description: The qualityControl field provides a location for the description of actions taken to either control or assess the quality of data resulting from the associated method step.

description.title: description
description.description: The field Description contains general textual descriptions.

sampling.title: sampling
sampling.description: Description of sampling procedures including the geographic, temporal and taxonomic coverage of the study.

studyExtent.title: studyExtent
studyExtent.description: The field studyExtent represents both a specific sampling area and the sampling frequency (temporal boundaries, frequency of occurrence). The geographic studyExtent is usually a surrogate (representative area of) for the larger area documented in the "studyAreaDescription".

description.title: description
description.description: The field Description contains general textual descriptions.

samplingDescription.title: samplingDescription
samplingDescription.description: The samplingDescription field allows for a text-based/human readable description of the sampling procedures used in the research project. The content of this element would be similar to a description of sampling procedures found in the methods section of a journal article.

para.title: para
para.description: The "paragraph" element allows for text blocks to be included in EML.

project.title: project
project.description: The project field contains information on the project in which this dataset was collected. It includes information such as project personnel, funding, study area, project design and related projects.

title.title: title
title.description: The 'title' field provides a description of the resource that is being documented that is long enough to differentiate it from other similar resources. Multiple titles may be provided, particularly when trying to express the title in more than one language (use the "xml:lang" attribute to indicate the language if not English/en).

personnel.title: personnel
personnel.description: The Personnel field extends ResponsibleParty with role information and is used to document people involved in a research project by providing contact information and their role in the project.

organizationName.title: organizationName
organizationName.description: The language in which the resource (not the metadata document) is written

individualName.description: The individualName field contains subfields so that a person's name can be broken down into parts.

When the party represents an organization, this is the URL to a website or other online information about the organization. If the party is an individual, it might be their personal web site or other related online information about the party.

role.title: role
role.description: Use this field to describe the role the party played with respect to the resource. Some potential roles include technician, reviewer, principal investigator, and many others.

funding.title: funding
funding.description: The funding field is used to provide information about funding sources for the project such as: grant and contract numbers; names and addresses of funding sources.

para.title: para
para.description: The "paragraph" element allows for text blocks to be included in EML.

studyAreaDescription.title: studyAreaDescription
studyAreaDescription.description: The studyAreaDescription field documents the physical area associated with the research project. It can include descriptions of the geographic, temporal, and taxonomic coverage of the research location and descriptions of domains (themes) of interest such as climate, geology, soils or disturbances.

descriptor.title: descriptor
descriptor.description: The descriptor field is used to document domains (themes) of interest such as climate, geology, soils or disturbances.

descriptorValue.title: descriptorValue
descriptorValue.description: The descriptorValue field contains a general description, either thematic or geographic, of the study area.

designDescription.title: designDescription
designDescription.description: The field designDescription contains general textual descriptions of research design. It can include detailed accounts of goals, motivations, theory, hypotheses, strategy, statistical design, and actual work.

description.title: description
description.description: The field Description contains general textual descriptions.
